handoff.salientSummary: Implemented Tauri native pubsub for eventa event contracts. Renderer eventa context subscriptions now bridge to Tauri listen() for electron:window:bounds, electron:screen:cursor-screen-point, and electron:auto-updater:state-changed. Renderer emits for those contracts route through Rust emit_event, which can broadcast to all windows, the current window, or a named window.
handoff.whatWasImplemented: Added packages/tauri-eventa/src/tauri/pubsub.ts with subscribeTauriEvent(), emitTauriEvent(), tauriEventNameFromContract(), and tauriEventPubSub(). Patched createContextFromTauriIpc() so context.on/context.once/context.off/context.emit recognize eventa:event:electron:* contracts and bridge them to Tauri native events. Added apps/stage-tauri/src/commands/events.rs with emit_event and registered it in commands/mod.rs and main.rs. Main Tauri setup now emits dirty-checked window bounds, a placeholder cursor point stream, and a one-shot auto-updater not-available state. Exported the pubsub helpers and added @tauri-apps/api to the package catalog.
handoff.whatWasLeftUndone: Cursor screen point is still a placeholder stream of { x: 0, y: 0 } until the Wayland cursor reader lands. Auto-updater state is a stub until the real updater binding feature replaces it. No interactive multi-window screenshot was captured in this headless session.

commandsRun.command: pnpm -F @proj-airi/tauri-eventa exec vitest run src/tauri/index.test.ts src/tauri/pubsub.test.ts

commandsRun.observation: tsdown built index, tauri/index, tauri/pubsub, and contract entries successfully.
Rust build completed successfully with the five known placeholder dead-code warnings from prior invoke-contracts work.

cases.name: registers a callback and the callback fires on emit
cases.verifies: eventa event contracts derive the correct Tauri channel and receive payloads from listen().

cases.name: returns an unsubscribe function that stops subsequent deliveries
cases.verifies: unsubscribe removes the JS callback from the channel bucket.

cases.name: multiple subscribers on the same channel all receive the payload
cases.verifies: one native listener fans out to multiple JS subscribers.

cases.name: calls the Rust emit_event command with the right channel
cases.verifies: emitTauriEvent() invokes the Rust broadcaster with target all.

cases.name: bridges eventa context.on subscriptions to Tauri native events
bridges native eventa context.emit calls to the Rust event broadcaster
cases.verifies: context.emit() for event contracts uses emit_event instead of raw IPC.

tests.coverage: Unit tests cover event name derivation, subscribe/unsubscribe, fan-out, emit routing, context.on bridging, and context.emit bridging. Rust command behavior is covered by cargo build only.

discoveredIssues.description: The mission directory contained a local API gateway key in runtime-custom-models.json. It was redacted before the docs mission move commit.
MCP Serena/jcodemunch tools required by AGENTS.md were not available in this runtime, so native git/rg/sed/pnpm/cargo/apply_patch tooling was used and recorded as fallback.

deviations.whatIDidInstead: Used native diff review, targeted vitest, typecheck, package build, cargo build, and git diff --check.
deviations.why: The MCP tools named by AGENTS.md were not exposed in the current tool list.
